items.question: Is Tracka free?

items.question: Is my data safe?

items.question: Does it work on mobile?

visibility.info: This configuration **does not change plans in Tracka** — it only controls what is displayed on the landing page for marketing purposes. Data comes from the actual Tracka configuration.

ownershipTransfer.title: Transfer Ownership
ownershipTransfer.subtitle: Hand over house administration to another user

content.intro: If you are the administrator of a house, you can transfer ownership to another registered Tracka user.

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.step1: Go to the house and open Members (Settings)
howItWorks.step2: In the 'Transfer Ownership' section, enter the new owner's email
howItWorks.step3: The recipient will receive a 6-digit code by email and a notification in the app
howItWorks.step4: The recipient enters the code on the house Members page to accept

rules.title: Important Rules
rules.rule1: The recipient must have a Tracka account
rules.rule2: Only one pending transfer per house is allowed
rules.rule4: You can cancel before the recipient accepts
rules.rule5: Once accepted, the transfer is irreversible

afterTransfer.title: What Happens After Transfer
afterTransfer.desc: The new owner becomes Administrator of the house. The previous owner stays as Editor — can view and edit items, but can no longer manage members.

ownerProtection.title: Owner Protection
ownerProtection.desc: The owner (house creator) cannot be removed or demoted by other administrators. They also cannot remove themselves from their own house. The only way to stop being the owner is to transfer ownership to another user.

ownerIsNotARole.title: Owner Is Not a Role
ownerIsNotARole.desc: "Owner" is not a role that can be manually assigned. It is automatically determined by who created the house. To make another user the owner — even if they are a Viewer — simply use the Ownership Transfer feature. The system handles everything: the recipient becomes Administrator and the previous owner becomes Editor, with no need to change roles beforehand.

content.warning: Warning: once accepted, the transfer cannot be undone!
content.tip: Cancel the transfer at any time before acceptance if you change your mind.

techDocs.title: Technical Documentation
techDocs.subtitle: Architecture, security and variables — SUPERADMIN only

content.intro: Complete technical documentation for the Tracka system. Only SUPERADMIN users can view this page.

email.name: Email
email.desc: Resend API for verification, reset and deletion

jwt.name: Session
jwt.desc: JWT HMAC-SHA256, HttpOnly + Secure + SameSite=Lax cookie

encryption.name: Sensitive Data
encryption.desc: AES-256-GCM for CPF, documents, phone and WhatsApp

superadmin.name: SUPERADMIN
superadmin.desc: Root. Full access to all data from all users and houses.

admin.name: ADMIN
admin.desc: Company admin. Admin panel, landing, API keys.

employee.name: EMPLOYEE
employee.desc: Platform role. Employee.role defines admin access.

auth.name: JWT_SECRET, GOOGLE_*
auth.desc: JWT secret and Google OAuth credentials

email.name: RESEND_API_KEY
email.desc: Resend API key for email sending

cloudinary.name: CLOUDINARY_*
cloudinary.desc: Cloud name, API key and secret

stripe.name: STRIPE_*
stripe.desc: Secret key, webhook secret and publishable key

content.warning: This documentation contains confidential information. Do not share outside the development team.

model.name: OwnershipTransfer
model.desc: Prisma model: id, houseId, fromUserId, toUserId, code (6 digits), status (PENDING/ACCEPTED/CANCELLED), expiresAt (48h)

actions.name: Server Actions
actions.desc: initiateTransferAction, cancelTransferAction, acceptTransferAction, getPendingTransferAction, getIncomingTransfersAction — in ownership-transfer.actions.ts

emails.name: Emails
emails.desc: ownershipTransferTemplate (code + link) and ownershipTransferCancelledTemplate — in email-templates.ts

flow.name: Flow
flow.desc: Admin initiates → 6-digit code via email + notification → recipient accepts (atomic tx: House.userId changes, new=ADMIN, old=EDITOR) → irreversible

ownerProtection.name: Owner Protection
ownerProtection.desc: House.userId cannot be removed or demoted. removeMemberAction checks member.userId === house.userId. UI hides Remove button via ownerId prop. Golden 'Owner' badge. Only exit: transfer via OwnershipTransfer.

devSetup.title: Dev Environment Setup
devSetup.subtitle: Clone, install and run the project locally — SUPERADMIN

content.intro: Complete guide to set up the Tracka development environment.

editor.name: VS Code
editor.desc: Recommended editor with ESLint and Prisma extensions

steps.title: Step by Step
steps.step3: Copy .env.example to .env and fill in the variables
steps.step4: Generate Prisma client: npx prisma generate
steps.step5: Start the server: npm run dev (access http://localhost:3000)

envVars.title: Environment Variables (.env)
envVars.desc: All variables needed to run locally:

auth.name: JWT_SECRET
auth.desc: Secret to sign JWT tokens (min. 32 chars)

encryption.name: ENCRYPTION_KEY
encryption.desc: AES-256 key for encrypting sensitive data (64 hex chars)

email.name: RESEND_API_KEY
email.desc: Resend API key for sending emails

cloudinary.name: CLOUDINARY_*
cloudinary.desc: CLOUD_NAME, API_KEY, API_SECRET for image uploads

content.tip: Use Prisma Studio (npx prisma studio) to inspect and edit data directly in the database during development.

devDatabase.title: Database Guide
devDatabase.subtitle: Prisma schema, migrations and scripts — SUPERADMIN

content.intro: Complete guide to the Tracka database: schema, migrations, Turso and utility scripts.

schema.title: Schema (Prisma Models)
schema.desc: The schema is at prisma/schema.prisma. Main models:

user.name: User
user.desc: System users (email, password, role, encrypted personal data)

item.name: Item
item.desc: Stored items (name, description, quantity, photo, status)

history.name: History
history.desc: Action log (CREATE, UPDATE, MOVE, DELETE, LOAN, RETURN)

isDefaultStrategy.title: isDefault Strategy ('Geral' Entities)
isDefaultStrategy.desc: A fundamental design decision: when creating a House, the system automatically creates a default Room 'Geral' and Furniture 'Geral', both with isDefault: true.

purpose.name: Purpose
purpose.desc: Every Item must belong to a Furniture in the database (Item → Furniture → Room → House). The 'Geral' entities allow storing items directly in a house or room without requiring the user to create an explicit furniture piece.

behavior.name: UI Behavior
behavior.desc: Entities with isDefault: true are HIDDEN in the interface. The user never sees 'Geral' in breadcrumbs, location labels, selects or lists. Use item.furniture?.isDefault and item.furniture?.room?.isDefault to filter.

creation.name: Automatic Creation
creation.desc: createHouse → creates Room 'Geral' (isDefault) + Furniture 'Geral' (isDefault). createRoom → creates Furniture 'Geral' (isDefault). This ensures a default location always exists for items.

queries.name: Queries
queries.desc: In listings, filter with isDefault: false to exclude default entities (e.g., listRooms, listFurniture). When displaying location, check isDefault before including the name in the label.

turso.title: Turso (LibSQL)
turso.desc: Tracka uses Turso as database, a distributed version of SQLite.
turso.step1: Create an account at turso.tech and a database
turso.step2: Copy the URL (libsql://...) and auth token to .env
turso.step3: In dev: npx prisma db push. In production: use migration scripts (scripts/)

scripts.title: Utility Scripts (scripts/)
scripts.desc: Node.js scripts for direct database operations:

migration.name: run_migration_*.mjs
migration.desc: Manual migration scripts for schema changes

backfill.name: backfill_*.mjs
backfill.desc: Fill new columns with default values

content.warning: Always backup before running migration or backfill scripts in production. Use npx dotenv-cli -e .env -- node scripts/name.mjs to load variables.

content.intro: Complete reference for the Tracka REST API routes. All under /api/*.

login.desc: Login with email and password → returns JWT + systemRole

register.desc: Register new user → sends verification email

me.desc: Returns logged-in user data (PUT to update, DELETE to remove)

confirmDelete.desc: Confirm account deletion via email token

create.desc: Create new house (multipart/form-data with optional image)

list.desc: List items (with filters: houseId, roomId, furnitureId)

move.desc: Move item between locations or toggle loan status

vision.desc: Identify objects by photo (Google Vision + Translate)

storage.desc: Generate HMAC signature for Cloudinary upload

content.tip: All authenticated routes check the JWT cookie. Use the Cookie header in Postman to test.

devDeploy.title: Deploy & Production
devDeploy.subtitle: Vercel, variables, Stripe and domain — SUPERADMIN

content.intro: Guide to deploy Tracka in production using Vercel + Turso + Cloudinary + Stripe.

vercel.title: Deploy to Vercel
vercel.desc: Tracka runs on Vercel with automatic builds on each GitHub push.
vercel.step1: Connect the GitHub repository on Vercel
vercel.step3: Build runs automatically: npm run build
vercel.step4: Check the deploy at Production → Domains

auth.name: JWT_SECRET + GOOGLE_*
auth.desc: JWT secret and OAuth (use different values from dev)

stripe.title: Configure Stripe Webhooks
stripe.desc: Stripe needs a webhook to notify about payment events.
stripe.step1: In Stripe Dashboard → Webhooks → Add endpoint

checklist.title: Production Checklist
checklist.step1: All environment variables configured on Vercel
checklist.step2: Custom domain configured with active SSL
checklist.step3: Stripe webhook pointing to production URL
checklist.step4: Send email configured in Resend (verified domain)
checklist.step5: Prisma schema synced with database via migration scripts (do not use prisma db push in production)

content.warning: Never use Stripe test keys in production. Always verify STRIPE_SECRET_KEY starts with sk_live_.

plansLimits.title: Plans & Limits
plansLimits.subtitle: Understand available plans and how limits work

content.intro: Tracka offers 4 plan types, each with specific limits and features. When subscribing, limits are frozen at the time of purchase.

trial.name: Trial
trial.desc: 15 days with all features. After the period, automatically migrates to Free.

free.name: Free
free.desc: Free plan with basic features and reduced limits.

plus.name: Plus
plus.desc: R$ 9.90/mo — more space, collaborators and advanced features.

pro.name: Pro
pro.desc: R$ 19.90/mo — maximum limits and all features enabled.

snapshot.title: Limits Snapshot
snapshot.desc: When subscribing to a paid plan, limits are frozen ("snapshot"). Even if admin changes plans later, existing subscribers keep their original limits.
snapshot.step1: User chooses a plan on the Pricing page
snapshot.step3: System saves an immutable snapshot of the limits at that moment

cancellation.title: Irreversible Cancellation
cancellation.desc: When canceling, the plan is permanently terminated. If re-subscribing, the user assumes current plans. There is no way to recover the previous plan.

currency.title: Currency Conversion
currency.desc: All prices are defined in BRL (Brazilian Real). For users accessing from other countries, Tracka automatically detects the local currency from the browser settings and displays an approximate converted price.

detection.name: Automatic Currency Detection
detection.desc: The system first checks if pt-BR (or pt) is present in your browser's language list. If so, you are identified as a Brazilian user and prices are always shown in USD ($), regardless of other languages configured (e.g., en-GB). Only when pt-BR is NOT in the list, the system detects the currency from the browser region (e.g., en-GB → GBP, de-DE → EUR). If no region is detected, the default is always USD ($).

liveRate.name: Live Exchange Rate
liveRate.desc: Exchange rates are fetched in real-time from AwesomeAPI and cached locally for performance. If the API is unavailable, the last cached rate is used as fallback.

display.name: How Prices Are Displayed
display.desc: Converted prices are shown as approximate values (e.g., ~$1.74/mo) alongside the original BRL price (R$ 9,90). A disclaimer indicates these are reference values only.

examples.name: Detection Examples
examples.desc: Browser [pt-BR, en-GB] → USD ($) (Brazilian user, en-GB ignored) | Browser [pt-BR, en] → USD ($) | Browser [en-GB] only → GBP (£) (genuinely from UK) | Browser [en-US] → USD ($) | Browser [de-DE] → EUR (€) | Browser [en-AU] → AUD (A$) | No locale detected → USD ($)

checkout.name: Final Amount at Checkout
checkout.desc: The final payment amount is calculated by Stripe in the buyer's local currency at the exact exchange rate at the time of payment. The displayed price is for reference only.

content.warning: Subscription cancellation is IRREVERSIBLE. Make sure to clearly communicate this to users.
content.tip: Configure plans in Admin → Landing Page → Plans and Limits. Changes only affect new subscribers!

paymentSecurity.title: Payment Security
paymentSecurity.subtitle: How Soluções RKM processes payments and protects your data

content.intro: Soluções RKM prioritizes the security of your financial data. We use Stripe, one of the world's largest payment processors, to ensure your transactions are secure, transparent and fully compliant with current regulations.

processor.title: Payment Processor
processor.desc: Stripe is used by companies like Google, Amazon, Shopify and Nubank. Your payment data is processed exclusively by Stripe — it never passes through our servers.

pciDss.name: PCI-DSS Level 1 Certification
pciDss.desc: Stripe holds the highest security certification in the credit card industry

noStorage.name: No card storage
noStorage.desc: Credit card data is never stored on our servers

encryption.name: End-to-end encryption
encryption.desc: All transactions are protected with TLS/HTTPS encryption

sca.name: Strong Authentication (SCA)
sca.desc: 3D Secure authentication support for additional fraud protection

googlePlay.title: Google Play Compliance
googlePlay.desc: Tracka is available as a progressive web app (PWA) and operates in full compliance with Google Play Store policies. Since 2025, Google officially allows alternative payment systems like Stripe in apps distributed through the Play Store.

alternative.name: Authorized alternative payment
alternative.desc: Google Play officially allows the use of Stripe and other payment processors, per updated 2025-2026 policies

global.name: Global compliance
global.desc: New policies are being rolled out across the US, Europe, Australia, Japan, South Korea and other regions, including Brazil

transparent.name: Regulatory transparency
transparent.desc: Soluções RKM follows Google's SAFE guidelines, ensuring security and trust for users

dataProtection.title: Data Protection
dataProtection.desc: Beyond payment security, we protect your personal data in compliance with Brazilian and international legislation.

lgpd.name: LGPD (Law 13.709/2018)
lgpd.desc: Data processing in full compliance with Brazil's General Data Protection Law

minimal.name: Minimal data collection
minimal.desc: We only collect data strictly necessary for the service to function

noSharing.name: No data selling
noSharing.desc: Your personal and financial data is never sold or shared for advertising purposes

transparency.title: Our Transparency Commitment
transparency.desc: Soluções RKM is committed to maintaining a transparent relationship with all users:
transparency.point1: Prices always clear and visible on the plans page, with no hidden charges
transparency.point2: Explicit acceptance of terms and conditions before any charge
transparency.point3: Cancellation available at any time, without bureaucracy
transparency.point4: Dedicated support for billing and subscription questions

content.info: For payment questions, contact suporte@solucoesrkm.com. To exercise your LGPD rights: privacidade@solucoesrkm.com.
content.tip: You can check your subscription status anytime in your profile Settings.

subscriptionFlow.title: Subscription Flow
subscriptionFlow.subtitle: How the subscription process, terms acceptance and legal recording works

content.intro: The subscription flow ensures legal compliance with mandatory terms acceptance and complete recording of all contracted conditions.

flow.title: Step by Step
flow.step3: Terms acceptance modal shows plan limits
flow.step4: User reads and checks Terms/Privacy checkbox
flow.step5: Clicks "Agree and Subscribe" → redirected to Stripe
flow.step6: After payment, system creates Subscription with snapshot and acceptance record

planSnapshot.name: Plan Snapshot
planSnapshot.desc: Complete copy of contracted limits and features

snapshot.title: Snapshot Immutability
snapshot.desc: The snapshot is a frozen JSON with all limits and features at contracting time. It is NEVER changed.

content.warning: Keep Terms and Privacy Policy updated. Consult a lawyer for legal content.

adminSubscriptions.title: Subscription Management (Admin)
adminSubscriptions.subtitle: How to view subscriptions, acceptances and snapshots

content.intro: The Admin → Subscriptions panel shows all subscription data including snapshots, terms acceptances and Stripe billing info.

access.title: How to access
access.step3: Page lists all subscriptions with status filters

details.title: Available information
details.desc: Click on a subscription to see expanded details:

terms.name: 📄 Terms Acceptance
terms.desc: Accepted version, date/time and device IP

history.name: ⏱️ History
history.desc: Creation date, cancellation and current plan

snapshot.name: 🔒 Snapshot
snapshot.desc: All frozen numeric limits and features

filters.title: Filters
filters.desc: Use top filters: All, Active only, or Canceled only.

content.tip: Acceptance and snapshot data are immutable records — legal proof of user agreement.

adminSettingsGuide.title: Settings Guide
adminSettingsGuide.subtitle: How to administer the corporate system and help center

content.intro: The Soluções RKM corporate site has an Admin panel (/admin/settings) for global configurations and a Help Editor (/admin/help-editor) for managing help content.

adminSettings.title: /admin/settings — Admin Panel
adminSettings.desc: Accessible by SUPERADMIN and ADMIN. Controls global settings:
adminSettings.landing: Texts, colors, images and sections of the landing page
adminSettings.apiKeys: API keys for external services (Google Vision, Cloudinary, etc.)
adminSettings.freshdesk: Support widget, tickets, Knowledge Base and chat — full Freshdesk configuration
adminSettings.plans: Plan configuration, limits and pricing (SUPERADMIN only)
adminSettings.team: Team management — add, remove and change member roles

helpEditor.title: /admin/help-editor — Help Editor
helpEditor.desc: Tool for editing help topic content without requiring a deploy. Only SUPERADMIN and ADMIN have access.
helpEditor.step2: Select a topic from the left sidebar
helpEditor.step3: Edit content using Markdown (bold, lists, sections, blockquotes)
helpEditor.step4: Use the preview button to toggle between editing and preview
helpEditor.step5: Click "Save" — content is updated instantly on the help pages
helpEditor.step6: To revert, click "Restore Default" — returns to original content

freshdeskSync.title: Sync Help with Freshdesk
freshdeskSync.desc: After editing topics, sync with Freshdesk to keep the Knowledge Base updated:
freshdeskSync.step1: Go to Admin → Freshdesk → Knowledge Base section
freshdeskSync.step3: The system creates/updates Categories, Folders and Articles automatically
freshdeskSync.step4: Public topics are visible to all; superadminOnly ones are set to "Agents Only"

permissions.title: Permissions by Role
permissions.desc: Each role has different access levels:
permissions.superadmin: Full access: manages settings, plans, team, help and Freshdesk
permissions.admin: Manages site settings and edits help content. Cannot edit plans
permissions.editor: Read-only access to admin panel. Cannot edit settings
permissions.viewer: Blocked from admin panel. Read-only access to public help

contentStrategy.title: Content Strategy
contentStrategy.desc: Help is split between two workspaces:
contentStrategy.corporate: solucoesrkm.com/help — corporate topics: plans, payment, admin settings, tech docs, Freshdesk
contentStrategy.tracka: Tracka (Controle das Coisas) — app-exclusive topics: features, how-to, usage tips

content.tip: To add a new topic: create the component in [slug]/page.tsx, register in help-topics.ts and add translations in messages/pt.json and en.json.

freshdeskSync.title: Sync Help → Freshdesk
freshdeskSync.subtitle: Automatic synchronization of help topics with the Freshdesk Knowledge Base

content.intro: Soluções RKM automatically syncs all corporate help topics with the Freshdesk Knowledge Base, maintaining a single source of truth and eliminating content duplication.

howItWorks.title: How it works
howItWorks.desc: The sync reads all corporate help topics, converts them to HTML and publishes to the Knowledge Base via Freshdesk API v2.
howItWorks.step1: Go to Admin → Freshdesk → Knowledge Base
howItWorks.step3: The system creates/updates Categories, Folders and Articles in Freshdesk
howItWorks.step4: Topics edited via help-editor are converted from Markdown to HTML

visibility.title: Visibility Control
visibility.desc: Article visibility is automatically set based on topic permissions:
visibility.public: Unrestricted topics — visible to all KB visitors
visibility.agents: superadminOnly topics — visible only to Freshdesk agents (internal information)

mapping.title: Persistent Mapping
mapping.desc: The system maintains an internal mapping (slug → Freshdesk ID) stored in SiteSettings. This allows re-syncs to update existing articles instead of creating duplicates.

api.title: API Endpoints
api.desc: Sync can be triggered via API (useful for automation):

content.warning: You must configure FRESHDESK_API_KEY and FRESHDESK_DOMAIN in environment variables before the first sync.
content.tip: After editing topics in help-editor, click "Sync KB" to publish changes to Freshdesk automatically!

freshdeskKb.title: Freshdesk — Customer Support
freshdeskKb.subtitle: Support module configuration: widget, tickets, Knowledge Base and chat

content.intro: Soluções RKM integrates with Freshworks to offer 4 customer support modules, all configurable from the admin panel.

widget.name: Support Widget
widget.desc: Floating bubble for quick access to FAQ and contact

tickets.name: Ticket Portal
tickets.desc: Dedicated page to create and track support tickets

kb.name: Knowledge Base
kb.desc: Knowledge base with help articles synced from internal Help

chat.name: Freshchat
chat.desc: Real-time chat with the support team

config.title: How to configure
config.step2: Enable desired modules with the toggle
config.step3: Fill in required fields (Widget ID, Token, URLs)

auth.title: Automatic identification
auth.desc: Widget and Freshchat modules can automatically identify the logged-in user. When enabled, the user's name and email are sent to Freshdesk.

content.tip: You can paste the entire Freshworks deploy script — the system automatically extracts the Widget ID and Chat Token!

faqLegal.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faqLegal.subtitle: Common questions about Tracka, plans and features

content.intro: Here are answers to the most frequently asked questions about Tracka.

q1.a: Tracka is a personal and business inventory management platform that lets you catalog, organize and locate your belongings with AI assistance.

q2.a: Yes! The Free plan is free forever with item and feature limits. For additional resources, there are Plus and Pro plans.

q3.q: Can I cancel my subscription anytime?
q3.a: Yes, you can cancel anytime. Access continues until the end of the paid period. Note: once cancelled, the previous plan cannot be recovered.

q4.a: Yes. We use HTTPS/TLS encryption, bcrypt password hashing, and follow LGPD. Payment data is processed exclusively by Stripe.

q5.a: Tracka uses Google Cloud Vision AI to analyze photos and suggest item names automatically. Images are temporarily processed and not stored by Google.

q6.a: Yes! You can invite other users to collaborate with different access levels (Admin, Editor, Viewer).

q7.q: What happens if I exceed my plan limits?
q7.a: New items cannot be created beyond the limit. Existing items remain accessible. Consider upgrading to a higher plan.

q8.a: Through the support widget, email suporte@solucoesrkm.com, or by opening a ticket in the support portal.

termosDeUso.title: Terms of Use
termosDeUso.subtitle: Terms and conditions of the Tracka platform

content.intro: By using Tracka, you agree to these Terms of Use. Please read carefully.

s1.title: 1. Acceptance of Terms
s1.desc: By accessing and using the Tracka platform, operated by Soluções RKM, you agree to these Terms of Use.

s2.title: 2. Service Description
s2.desc: Tracka is a SaaS platform for personal and business inventory management with AI-powered cataloging.

s3.title: 3. Plans and Subscriptions
s3.desc: Available in Free, Plus and Pro plans. Upon subscribing, limits are frozen (snapshot). Later changes do not affect existing subscribers. Cancellation is permanent.

s4.title: 4. Payments
s4.desc: Processed by Stripe. Charges are recurring and monthly unless cancelled. We do not store credit card data.

s5.title: 5. Acceptable Use
s5.desc: Users must provide truthful information, not share credentials, not use the service for illegal purposes.

s6.title: 6. Intellectual Property
s6.desc: Tracka, including source code and design, is exclusive property of Soluções RKM. User data remains user property.

s7.title: 7. Limitation of Liability
s7.desc: The Service is provided "as is". We are not responsible for data loss from misuse or decisions based on AI analysis.

s8.title: 8. Data Protection
s8.desc: Data collection follows LGPD (Law 13.709/2018). See our Privacy Policy for details.

s9.title: 9. Right of Withdrawal
s9.desc: Users can withdraw within 7 calendar days (Art. 49 of CDC), by email request.

s10.title: 10. Changes
s10.desc: We reserve the right to modify these Terms. Changes are communicated via email and/or platform notification.

content.warning: These terms are governed by the laws of the Federative Republic of Brazil.

privacidade.title: Privacy Policy
privacidade.subtitle: How we collect, use and protect your personal data

content.intro: This policy describes how we handle your personal data in compliance with LGPD (Law 13.709/2018).

s1.title: 1. Data Collected
s1.desc: We collect registration data (name, email), inventory (items, photos), browsing (IP, browser) and terms acceptance.

s2.title: 2. Payment Data
s2.desc: Processed exclusively by Stripe. We do not store credit card data.

s3.title: 3. Artificial Intelligence
s3.desc: Images sent to Google Cloud Vision are temporary and not stored after processing.

s4.title: 4. Purposes
s4.desc: Service provision, payment processing, legal compliance, experience improvement and notifications.

s5.title: 5. Legal Basis
s5.desc: Consent, contract execution, legitimate interest and legal obligation.

s6.title: 6. Sharing
s6.desc: Data shared with Stripe, Google Cloud, Cloudinary, Turso and Vercel. We do not sell personal data.

s7.title: 7. Security
s7.desc: Bcrypt passwords, HTTPS/TLS, immutable snapshots, acceptance with IP/timestamp, redundant backups.

s8.title: 8. Your Rights (LGPD)
s8.desc: Access, correction, anonymization, portability, deletion and consent revocation. Email: privacidade@solucoesrkm.com

s9.title: 9. Retention
s9.desc: Account data kept while active. Acceptances permanent for audit. Subscription data for 5 years (tax obligation).

s10.title: 10. Cookies
s10.desc: We use only essential cookies for authentication and session. No advertising tracking.

cookies.title: Cookie Preferences
cookies.subtitle: Understand which cookies we use and how to manage them

content.intro: Tracka uses cookies to ensure proper platform functionality.

types.title: Cookie Types
types.essential: Required for authentication, session and security. Cannot be disabled.
types.analytics: Used to understand how users interact with the platform (currently not used).
types.functional: Store preferences like theme (light/dark) and selected language.

manage.title: How to manage
manage.desc: You can manage cookies in your browser settings. Note that disabling essential cookies may prevent platform use.

content.tip: Tracka does not use advertising tracking cookies. Your privacy is a priority!

avisosLegais.title: Legal Notices
avisosLegais.subtitle: Legal bases, compliance and regulatory notices

content.intro: Tracka operates in compliance with Brazilian legislation. Below are the main applicable legal frameworks.

lgpd.title: LGPD — General Data Protection Law
lgpd.desc: Law 13.709/2018. Regulates personal data processing. Tracka records explicit consent, offers data portability.

cdc.title: CDC — Consumer Protection Code
cdc.desc: Law 8.078/1990. We guarantee 7-day right of withdrawal, price transparency and consumer service.

marco.title: Marco Civil da Internet
marco.desc: Law 12.965/2014. We guarantee network neutrality, communication privacy and data transparency.

propriedade.title: Intellectual Property
propriedade.desc: Tracka, including source code, design and brands, is exclusive property of Soluções RKM.

content.warning: All terms, policies and legal notices are subject to change with at least 15 days advance notice.

autoTranslation.title: Auto Translation
autoTranslation.subtitle: How the automatic translation system works

content.intro: The automatic translation system allows content edited in the admin panel to be automatically translated when saved. It uses the Google Translate REST API (free).

howItWorks.title: How it works
howItWorks.desc: When you edit and save fields in the admin panel, the system automatically detects which fields were changed and translates only those fields to the other language.
howItWorks.step1: Edit the desired fields in the settings page (hero, testimonials, FAQ, footer, etc.)
howItWorks.step2: Click Save — the system saves first and then starts automatic translation
howItWorks.step3: Translated fields are saved to the other language configuration automatically

locale.name: Language
locale.desc: Language is controlled by the URL (/pt or /en). Editing in /pt saves in Portuguese and translates to English.

trigger.name: When it translates
trigger.desc: The translator is only called when a field is edited and saved. Unchanged fields do not trigger calls.

idempotent.name: Cache
idempotent.desc: If content is already correctly translated in both languages, the translator is never called again.

success.name: Success
success.desc: Field translated and saved successfully. No action needed.

warning.name: Warning
warning.desc: Partial translation — some fields failed. Check the yellow panel below the Save button.

error.name: Error
error.desc: Translation service unavailable or database inaccessible. Try again later.

migrateAll.desc: One-time migration — translates entire config from one language to another. SUPERADMIN only.

translateFields.desc: On-demand translation — translates specific changed fields. Called automatically on save.

technical.title: Technical details
technical.dbPt: PT config is saved as landing_page_config_pt in the database
technical.dbEn: EN config is saved as landing_page_config_en in the database
technical.fields: Translated fields: title, subtitle, CTA, sections, testimonials, FAQ, footer
technical.concurrency: Concurrency limit: 5 fields translated in parallel

content.warning: The free Google Translate API may have usage limits. For high-volume production, consider using a paid API.
content.tip: Always edit in the primary language (Portuguese) and let the system automatically translate to the second language.
